title: triticum vulgare subsp. dicoccum
intro: Triticum vulgare subsp. dicoccum, also known as emmer wheat, is an ancient and nutritious type of wheat that is a subspecies of common wheat. It is a diploid wheat with two sets of chromosomes, and its seeds have a higher nutritional content compared to modern wheat varieties. The medicinal properties of emmer wheat include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, regulate blood sugar, and have anti-inflammatory effects, making it beneficial for heart health and digestive issues. Emmer wheat can be consumed as a whole grain, used in bread and pasta production, or prepared as herbal teas and infusions, offering various health benefits and uses in traditional medicine.
